mnemonics: K-MEANS: Keep Moving Each Assignment 'til No Significant change (for the acronym and the iterative process)
Centroid Cinematics: The movie where data points find their closest celebrity center.
K Clusters? Karefully Choose, or Centers Create Chaos! (to remember the importance of choosing the right K)
keyPoints: The algorithm aims to partition n observations into k clusters where each observation belongs to the cluster with the nearest mean.
It is an iterative algorithm that alternates between two steps: assignment of points to the nearest centroid, and updating the centroids to be the mean of their assigned points.
The algorithm is sensitive to the initial placement of centroids and the choice of k, which is often determined using methods like the elbow method.
commonMistakes: Assuming the algorithm will always converge to the global optimum; it can get stuck in local minima, so multiple runs are recommended.
Using k-means on non-globular clusters or clusters of very different densities, as it performs poorly in these scenarios.
Forgetting to standardize the data when features are on different scales, which can distort the distance calculations and ruin the results.
